title: Decadent Brazilian Chicken Stir Fry
description: A low fat Brazilian dish featuring makrut lime leaves and pickle, this stir fry is a bitter and springy indulgence, made in a wok with chicken and vegetables.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a wok over medium high heat.
Add the chicken to the wok and cook until browned and cooked through.
Add the onion and bell pepper to the wok and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the makrut lime leaves, pickle, snow peas, salt, and black pepper to the wok and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Serve the stir fry hot, garnished with additional makrut lime leaves if desired.
Reheat the leftovers in the wok or in the microwave until hot and steaming.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
